ops.text: golangci-lint pinned at `version: latest` (golangci-lint-action @v9, fleet convention) produces NONDETERMINISTIC staticcheck SA5011 ("possible nil pointer dereference") false-positives on the standard Go test idiom: `got, err := Load(...); if err != nil { t.Fatalf(...) }; if got == nil { t.Fatal(...) }` followed by `got.Field` derefs. The finding appears and disappears across CI runs on effectively identical code. Evidence (cc-context, 2026-07-22): internal/web/store_test.go was byte-identical across commits 28915538 (16:02, CI PASS) and two prior runs at 15:20/14:30 (CI FAIL) and 298d6998 (16:08, CI FAIL on SA5011 at store_test.go:165/169/284/287) — yet locally golangci-lint v2.12.2 (the exact version CI installed), same .golangci.yml, run BOTH single-package and whole-module, reported 0 issues every time, and staticcheck@latest was clean; no platform/build-tag files exist to explain a linux-vs-darwin split. So the code is valid and the analyzer flakes. KNOWN FIX (code-shape, deterministic): extract a `mustLoad(t, url, model) *Page` test helper that asserts err/nil and RETURNS a guaranteed non-nil value, then replace the standalone nil-check-then-deref blocks with `got := mustLoad(...)` — the call sites then have no nil-check-before-deref for SA5011 to anchor on, and the helper's `return got` (post nil-check) is not a dereference, so the finding cannot fire regardless of the flake. Exemplar: cc-context commit e39565c9 (internal/web/store_test.go), which added mustLoad and turned CI green. This can recur on ANY fleet repo carrying the standalone nil-check-then-deref test idiom; per the fleet golangci retrofit invariant the remedy is ALWAYS to remove the code-shape anchor (a mustLoad-style helper or the file's combined `if err != nil || got == nil` guard), NEVER to pin or weaken golangci-lint config.
